ncert_truth_statements: A Wheatstone bridge consists of four resistors P, Q, R, and S arranged in a diamond (bridge) configuration with a galvanometer across the middle
The bridge is balanced when P/Q = R/S; at balance, no current flows through the galvanometer
A null or zero deflection of the galvanometer indicates the bridge is balanced and the ratio of resistances is satisfied
The Wheatstone bridge is used to measure an unknown resistance accurately by adjusting a known variable resistance until the galvanometer reads zero
When the bridge is unbalanced, a current flows through the galvanometer whose direction and magnitude indicate the degree of imbalance
